# mxroute_forwarder (Resource)

Manages an email forwarder (alias) on a mail domain. MXroute exposes no in-place update for a forwarder, so changing any attribute replaces the resource.

## Example Usage

```terraform
resource "mxroute_forwarder" "example" {
  domain       = "example.com"
  alias        = "sales"
  destinations = ["owner@example.net", "team@example.net"]
}
```

<!-- schema generated by tfplugindocs -->
## Schema

### Required

- `alias` (String) The local part of the forwarding address (e.g. `sales` for `sales@example.com`).
- `destinations` (Set of String) The email addresses mail to this alias is forwarded to. MXroute exposes no in-place update, so changing the destinations replaces the resource.
- `domain` (String) The domain the forwarder belongs to (e.g. `example.com`).

### Read-Only

- `email` (String) The full forwarding address (e.g. `sales@example.com`).
- `id` (String) Resource identifier — `<domain>/<alias>`.

## Import

Import is supported using the following syntax:

The [`terraform import` command](https://developer.hashicorp.com/terraform/cli/commands/import) can be used, for example:

```shell
# A forwarder is imported by its domain and alias, joined with a slash.
terraform import mxroute_forwarder.example example.com/sales
```
